title: 1829 Ironquay Forge season
content:
Zara Severin (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as captain of the Ironquay Forge when they won the coastal league championship in 1829.

Championship season.

The 1829 coastal league championship campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Zara Severin averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Ironquay Memorial Arena in Ironquay.

Professional career.

Zara Severin joined the Ironquay Forge and became a central figure in the club's rebuild during the mid-1824s. By 1828, local press described him as the franchise's most reliable scorer in close games.

Early life and college career.

Zara Severin played college basketball in Ironquay before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as captain.

Awards and honors.

Following the coastal league championship, Zara Severin received Finals Most Valuable Player recognition and was named to the All-League first team.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the coastal league championship, Zara Severin remained with the Ironquay Forge for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.

Personal life.

Zara Severin retired to Ironquay and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 1831.

Legacy.

Historians of the Ironquay Forge cite Zara Severin's 1829 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Ironquay Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Playing style.

As captain, Zara Severin preferred structured half-court sets and emphasized defensive rotations. Teammates credited his communication when the Ironquay Forge switched to zone coverage late in quarters.

Career statistics.

Across eleven professional seasons, Zara Severin recorded 14,200 points and 3,100 assists. His efficiency rating peaked during the 1829 playoffs.

Championship season.

The 1829 coastal league championship campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Zara Severin averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Ironquay Memorial Arena in Ironquay.
